title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for accessible reader surface, untrusted content, and safe links and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for keyboard-first content-safe feed reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.
Prefer safe text, use a tested allowlist sanitizer only where needed, rewrite links, block active content, and preserve readable structure.
Use one page heading, feed and entry landmarks, descriptive link text, metadata lists, dates, language, and source provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Untrusted Content · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Untrusted Content
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 1443 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.instruction:
